**Yinzhou** ([Chinese](/wiki/Simplified_Chinese_characters "Simplified Chinese characters"): 鄞州; [pinyin](/wiki/Pinyin "Pinyin"): *Yínzhōu*) is a district and the seat of the major city of [Ningbo](/wiki/Ningbo "Ningbo"), [Zhejiang](/wiki/Zhejiang "Zhejiang") province, [China](/wiki/China "China").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yinzhou,_Ningbo&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

In 220 BC, [Qin Shi Huang](/wiki/Qin_Shi_Huang "Qin Shi Huang"), the first emperor of the [Qin dynasty](/wiki/Qin_dynasty "Qin dynasty"), established three counties called Yin ([鄞](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%84%9E#Chinese "wikt:鄞")), Mao ([鄮](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%84%AE#Traditional_Chinese "wikt:鄮")) and Gouzhang (句章).[[1]](#cite_note-1) Later they were merged into Gouzhang county during the [Sui dynasty](/wiki/Sui_dynasty "Sui dynasty"). It was renamed Mao county during the [Tang dynasty](/wiki/Tang_dynasty "Tang dynasty"). It had assumed its current name of "Yin" in the [Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms period](/wiki/Five_Dynasties_and_Ten_Kingdoms_period "Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms period"). The city of [Ningbo](/wiki/Ningbo "Ningbo") was administrated by Yin county until after the establishment of the [People's Republic of China](/wiki/People%27s_Republic_of_China "People's Republic of China"). At the same time, Yin county became a county of Ningbo city. On April 19, 2002, it was renamed Yinzhou District. It is one of the few counties that has kept the same name since its establishment more than 2000 years ago.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Administrative divisions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yinzhou,_Ningbo&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Administrative divisions")]

Subdistricts:[[2]](#cite_note-2)

* [Shounan Subdistrict](/w/index.php?title=Shounan_Subdistrict&action=edit&redlink=1 "Shounan Subdistrict (page does not exist)") (首南街道), [Meixu Subdistrict](/w/index.php?title=Meixu_Subdistrict&action=edit&redlink=1 "Meixu Subdistrict (page does not exist)") (梅墟街道), [Shiqi Subdistrict](/w/index.php?title=Shiqi_Subdistrict,_Ningbo&action=edit&redlink=1 "Shiqi Subdistrict, Ningbo (page does not exist)") (石碶街道), [Xiaying Subdistrict](/w/index.php?title=Xiaying_Subdistrict&action=edit&redlink=1 "Xiaying Subdistrict (page does not exist)") (下应街道), [Zhonggongmiao Subdistrict](/w/index.php?title=Zhonggongmiao_Subdistrict&action=edit&redlink=1 "Zhonggongmiao Subdistrict (page does not exist)") (钟公庙街道), [Zhonghe Subdistrict](/w/index.php?title=Zhonghe_Subdistrict&action=edit&redlink=1 "Zhonghe Subdistrict (page does not exist)") (中河街道)

Towns:

* [Dongqianhu](/w/index.php?title=Dongqianhu&action=edit&redlink=1 "Dongqianhu (page does not exist)") (东钱湖镇), [Dongqiao](/wiki/Dongqiao,_Ningbo "Dongqiao, Ningbo") (洞桥镇), [Dongwu](/w/index.php?title=Dongwu&action=edit&redlink=1 "Dongwu (page does not exist)") (东吴镇), [Gulin](/w/index.php?title=Gulin,_Ningbo&action=edit&redlink=1 "Gulin, Ningbo (page does not exist)") (古林镇), [Gaoqiao](/w/index.php?title=Gaoqiao,_Ningbo&action=edit&redlink=1 "Gaoqiao, Ningbo (page does not exist)") (高桥镇), [Hengxi](/w/index.php?title=Hengxi,_Ningbo&action=edit&redlink=1 "Hengxi, Ningbo (page does not exist)") (横溪镇), [Hengjie](/w/index.php?title=Hengjie&action=edit&redlink=1 "Hengjie (page does not exist)") (横街镇), [Jitugang](/w/index.php?title=Jitugang&action=edit&redlink=1 "Jitugang (page does not exist)") (集士港镇), [Jiangshan](/w/index.php?title=Jiangshan,_Ningbo&action=edit&redlink=1 "Jiangshan, Ningbo (page does not exist)") (姜山镇), [Qiu'ai](/w/index.php?title=Qiu%27ai&action=edit&redlink=1 "Qiu'ai (page does not exist)") (邱隘镇), [Tangxi](/w/index.php?title=Tangxi,_Ningbo&action=edit&redlink=1 "Tangxi, Ningbo (page does not exist)") (塘溪镇), [Wuxiang](/w/index.php?title=Wuxiang,_Ningbo&action=edit&redlink=1 "Wuxiang, Ningbo (page does not exist)") (五乡镇), [Xianxiang](/w/index.php?title=Xianxiang&action=edit&redlink=1 "Xianxiang (page does not exist)") (咸祥镇), [Yinjiang](/w/index.php?title=Yinjiang,_Ningbo&action=edit&redlink=1 "Yinjiang, Ningbo (page does not exist)") (鄞江镇), [Yunlong](/w/index.php?title=Yunlong,_Ningbo&action=edit&redlink=1 "Yunlong, Ningbo (page does not exist)") (云龙镇), [Zhanqi](/w/index.php?title=Zhanqi&action=edit&redlink=1 "Zhanqi (page does not exist)") (瞻岐镇), [Zhangshui](/w/index.php?title=Zhangshui&action=edit&redlink=1 "Zhangshui (page does not exist)") (章水镇)

The only township is [Longguan Township](/wiki/Longguan_Township,_Ningbo "Longguan Township, Ningbo") (龙观乡)

## Historical personalities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yinzhou,_Ningbo&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Historical personalities")]

* [Cheng Duanli](/wiki/Cheng_Duanli "Cheng Duanli"), educator
* [Tong Dizhou](/wiki/Tong_Dizhou "Tong Dizhou"), embryologist

## Tourism

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yinzhou,_Ningbo&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Tourism")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c7/Ningbo_Tiantong_Si_2013.07.28_16-11-13.jpg/250px-Ningbo_Tiantong_Si_2013.07.28_16-11-13.jpg)](/wiki/File:Ningbo_Tiantong_Si_2013.07.28_16-11-13.jpg)

The Hall of [Skanda](/wiki/Skanda_(Buddhism) "Skanda (Buddhism)") in [Tiantong Temple](/wiki/Tiantong_Temple "Tiantong Temple").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/78/Sarira_Hall_of_Ashoka_Temple_in_Ningbo.JPG/250px-Sarira_Hall_of_Ashoka_Temple_in_Ningbo.JPG)](/wiki/File:Sarira_Hall_of_Ashoka_Temple_in_Ningbo.JPG)

[King Ashoka Temple](/wiki/King_Ashoka_Temple "King Ashoka Temple")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/2/2c/Ningbo_Yinzhou_Southern_CBD_view_from_skyscraper.jpg/250px-Ningbo_Yinzhou_Southern_CBD_view_from_skyscraper.jpg)](/wiki/File:Ningbo_Yinzhou_Southern_CBD_view_from_skyscraper.jpg)

Ningbo Southern Business District seen from a neighboring skyscraper.

* *[East Zhejiang Maritime Affairs and Folk Customs Museum](/wiki/East_Zhejiang_Maritime_Affairs_and_Folk_Customs_Museum "East Zhejiang Maritime Affairs and Folk Customs Museum")* (浙东海事民俗博物馆), housed in a former temple of Mazu and guildhall in central Ningbo, preserves the area's rich history of traditional arts and maritime trade.
* *[Ningbo Museum](/wiki/Ningbo_Museum "Ningbo Museum")* (宁波博物馆) is designed by [Wang Shu](/wiki/Wang_Shu "Wang Shu"), the first Chinese citizen to win the [Pritzker Architecture Prize](/wiki/Pritzker_Architecture_Prize "Pritzker Architecture Prize") in 2012. The museum focuses on Ningbo area history and traditional customs.
* *Liangzhu Cultural Park* (梁祝文化公园) was built to commemorate the beautiful love story of Liang Shanbo and Zhu Yingtai. The story is based on the Chinese legend of the [Butterfly Lovers](/wiki/Butterfly_Lovers "Butterfly Lovers").
* *Tiantong Scenic Spot* (天童风景名胜区) mainly consists of [Tiantong Temple](/wiki/Tiantong_Temple "Tiantong Temple") and Tiantong National Forest Park. Tiantong Temple is one of the most important [Chan](/wiki/Chan_Buddhism "Chan Buddhism") [Buddhist temples](/wiki/Buddhist_temples "Buddhist temples"); [Sōtō](/wiki/S%C5%8Dt%C5%8D "Sōtō") began its formation there under [Rujing](/wiki/Rujing "Rujing"). It was originally built during the [Western Jin dynasty](/wiki/Western_Jin_dynasty "Western Jin dynasty") around 300 AD (some date it between 265 and 316). It ranks second among the five sacred Chinese Zen Buddhist mountains. A very large complex of buildings, its former total of 999 rooms has now shrunk to 730 today, arranged in twenty groups of buildings rising up the mountain slope.
* *[Eyuwang Temple](/wiki/Eyuwang_Temple "Eyuwang Temple")* (阿育王寺) was built in A.D. 232. It is the only existing temple named after [Ashoka](/wiki/Ashoka "Ashoka") in China.
* *[Dongqian Lake](/wiki/Dongqian_Lake "Dongqian Lake")* (东钱湖) lies in the southeast of Yinzhou District. It is the largest natural freshwater lake in Zhejiang province, with a water surface area of 20 square kilometers. Since ancient times, the Lake has been a famous scenic spot in Eastern Zhejiang.
* *[Romon U-Park](/wiki/Romon_U-Park "Romon U-Park")* (罗蒙环球乐园) is one of the largest urban indoor amusement parks in the world.

## Industry

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yinzhou,_Ningbo&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Industry")]

Yinzhou District is home to more than 15,000 industrial organizations. The economy mainly consists of six sectors: light textiles, garments, machinery, electronics, automobile parts and food. In 2008, its GDP reached RMB 65.08 billion yuan and per capita GDP reached RMB 82,052 yuan (US$11,815). Its imports and exports totaled US$1.44 billion and US$6.6 billion, respectively.

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yinzhou,_Ningbo&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Economy")]

[Ningbo Southern Business District](/wiki/Ningbo_Southern_Business_District "Ningbo Southern Business District") ("Ningbo CBD") is located in the district. AUX Group[[3]](#cite_note-3) and Yinzhou Bank[[4]](#cite_note-4) are based in Yinzhou district.

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yinzhou,_Ningbo&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Education")]

Ningbo Higher Education Zone (宁波高等教育园区) is located at the south of Yinzhou District. Educational institutions located in the Higher Educational Zone:[[5]](#cite_note-5)

* Ningbo Institute of Technology
* [University of Nottingham Ningbo China](/wiki/University_of_Nottingham_Ningbo_China "University of Nottingham Ningbo China")
* Yinzhou Education Center
* Zhejiang University and Ningbo University Hygiene Vocational School
* [Zhejiang Wanli University](/wiki/Zhejiang_Wanli_University "Zhejiang Wanli University")

International schools in Yinzhou:[[6]](#cite_note-6)

* HD Ningbo School Yinzhou Campus
* [Ningbo Huamao International School](/wiki/Ningbo_Huamao_International_School "Ningbo Huamao International School")
